rulings.Case Title: ANGELA TICKLER VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A, A BUSINESS ENTITY
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT RESIDENTIAL CREDIT SOLUTIONS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend: (1) to allege duty, i.e. facts showing that defendant acted outside the scope of conventional money lending; and (2) to allege actionable violations of CC 2924 and tender. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA TICKLER VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A, A BUSINESS ENTITY
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON FKA THE BANK OF NEW and BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend: (1) to allege fraud with requisite particularity as to modification process; (2) to allege duty, i.e. facts showing that defendant acted outside the scope of conventional money lending; (3) to allege outrageous conduct and severe emotional distress; (4) to allege actionable violations of 2924 and tender. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD LEAK VS. FIRST AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL ZION CHURCH OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT FIRST AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL ZION CHURCH and LEROY HOGG's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges oppression and conscious disregard of his rights. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD LEAK VS. FIRST AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL ZION CHURCH OF et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT FIRST AFRICAN METHODIST EPISCOPAL ZION CHURCH amd LEROY HOGG's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges continuing violations up to August 19, 2013. While Plaintiff's recovery may be limited by the applicable statutes of limitations, Plaintiff's complaint filed on September 27, 2013 is not barred. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges IIED cause of action. Cause of action for UCL violation seeking restitution is sufficiently alleged. Defendant's argument regarding nuisance cause of action is not supported by any authority. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EI REAL ESTATE, INC. VS. MARILOU SAMSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication; Sep. Set Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 17, 2014, line 6. PLAINTIFF El REAL ESTATE, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. Plaintiff shifted burden. Defendant failed to provide competent evidence creating triable issue of fact. = (501/REQ)
